citation: Boulder County Community Justice Services / Domestic Abuse Prevention Project. 2008 Domestic Violence Statistical Report
legal_regime: Colorado DV framework; local coordinated DAPP response
sampling_frame: Boulder County law-enforcement reports received by DAPP
sample_size: 2008 total formal cases 1229; total informal cases 637
sex_or_gender_measurement: defendant sex/gender as reported in DAPP tables; extraction pending full table normalization
relationship_definition: intimate partner/domestic violence per DAPP/local reporting definitions
outcome: formal/informal reports, arrests, double arrests, sentencing/dismissal summary
primary_estimate: 2008 summary reports female arrest rate 25%; double arrests 97 cases/8% of charged cases; sentenced cases 25% female and 75% male; dismissal 33% for female sentenced cases and 16% for male sentenced cases
covariates: agency and report type in tables; limited case-mix controls in public report
missing_data_treatment: not fully described in extracted text
main_conclusion: Historical local system reported rising formal/informal cases, female arrests, and double arrests in 2008
limitations: Historical, DAPP-received reports only, definitions changed over time, no adjusted causal model
risk_of_bias: moderate/high for treatment inference; useful for historical descriptive context
applicability_to_boulder: high historical/local relevance, low current-policy directness
replication_materials: PDF downloaded and text extracted in data/raw_public/literature and data/interim/literature

citation: Female Domestic Violence Arrests in a Pro-Arrest Jurisdiction
study_dates: historical DAPP police-report data; exact dates pending full extraction
legal_regime: strong mandatory/pro-arrest Boulder County policy context
sampling_frame: police reports from 8 Boulder County law-enforcement agencies collected by DAPP
sample_size: 1,988 cases involving female suspects compared to 6,964 cases involving male suspects; multivariate analysis on 239 female-suspect cases
sex_or_gender_measurement: suspect sex categories in police/DAPP data
relationship_definition: domestic violence cases in DAPP police-report data
primary_estimate: abstract/extracted text reports formal police response in 68% of cases involving female suspects and 81% of cases involving male suspects; female suspects were less likely to be arrested than male suspects
covariates: age, race, prior DV, children, relationship status, injury/weapon variables pending full extraction
controls_pre_or_post_decision: mostly pre-decision incident/suspect variables in arrest models
main_conclusion: Boulder officers responded differently to male and female suspects; predictors of arrest differed by suspect sex
limitations: Historical data; possible selection/reporting bias; observational; reliability and legal-sufficiency coding require review
risk_of_bias: moderate for adjusted association; not causal proof
replication_materials: PDF downloaded and text extracted in data/raw_public/literature and data/interim/literature

citation: Hirschel et al. Explaining the Prevalence, Context, and Consequences of Dual Arrest in Intimate Partner Cases. NIJ final report
jurisdiction: United States NIBRS and selected jurisdictions
study_dates: NIBRS-era national data; dates pending full extraction
legal_regime: mandatory, preferred, and discretionary arrest-law comparison
sampling_frame: NIBRS assault/intimate-partner and comparison cases plus selected sites
sample_size: large national NIBRS sample; specific analytic Ns pending full extraction
relationship_definition: NIBRS relationship approximations for intimate partner and comparison relationships
outcome: no arrest, single arrest, dual arrest, prosecution/court consequences
primary_estimate: executive text reports overall dual arrest rate 1.3%; mandatory arrest laws increased dual-arrest likelihood; dual arrests more likely in intimate partner cases
covariates: law type, offense seriousness, victim/offender sex, agency/context variables
controls_pre_or_post_decision: police-stage models use incident/legal context; court-stage analysis uses later data
main_conclusion: Mandatory arrest can increase dual arrest; primary-aggressor provisions may mitigate dual arrest; consequences vary downstream
limitations: NIBRS relationship proxy; observational law/policy inference; reporting participation selection
applicability_to_boulder: moderate; informs mandatory-arrest and opposing-complaint design
funding_conflicts: National Institute of Justice grant report

citation: Klein. Practical Implications of Current Domestic Violence Research. NIJ report
sampling_frame: published studies and national data sources
outcome: reporting, arrest, prosecution, conviction, sentencing, treatment implications
primary_estimate: extracted text emphasizes that arrest and prosecution rates vary widely by jurisdiction and that police reports/arrests are imperfect proxies for underlying victimization
main_conclusion: Policy implications depend on stage and sampling frame; reported police data differ from victimization surveys and prosecution data
limitations: Narrative synthesis; estimates not pooled for Boulder-specific inference
risk_of_bias: moderate for generalization; useful conceptual framing
applicability_to_boulder: moderate; informs interpretation and funnel design
